county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Steubenville. One of Ohio's original counties (1797). Steubenville is one of Ohio's oldest cities; Dean Martin birthplace. Located on the upper Ohio River. Jefferson County Historical Association. Early Quaker settlements — Mount Pleasant (1800s) was a center of abolitionism. Iron and steel industry records; Wheeling-Steubenville industrial corridor. Strong immigrant communities: Irish, Italian, Greek, and Eastern European. Cross-reference with Brooke County, WV across the river.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1797. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1797. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.source_name: Jefferson County Will and Probate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1797. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Jefferson County Court of Common Pleas Records
source_profiles.repository: Jefferson County Clerk of Courts, Steubenville
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1797. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Jefferson County Auditor, Steubenville / Ohio History Connection
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. Ohio county auditor records from 1797. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes before 1820.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Ohio History Connection on microfilm

source_profiles.repository: Jefferson County Clerk of Courts, Steubenville / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Court of Common Pleas. Post-1906 copies at NARA. Ohio had massive European immigration — German, Irish, Welsh, English, Italian, Hungarian, Slovak, Polish, Czech, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Ohio naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and Abolitionist Records — Jefferson County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / National Underground Railroad Freedom Center
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio was a free state under the Northwest Ordinance (1787). Court records include fugitive slave cases, abolitionist society minutes, and related legal proceedings. Anti-slavery newspapers published in Ohio are valuable genealogical sources.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered across local historical societies and Ohio History Connection

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio provided approximately 320,000 soldiers to the Union Army — third most of any state. Roster Commission records, enlistment papers, discharge records, and pension files. Ohio History Connection holds the Ohio Roster Commission records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Ohio Roster Commission published 'Official Roster of the Soldiers of the State of Ohio in the War of the Rebellion' (12 volumes)

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Mount Vernon. Created 1808 from Fairfield. Kenyon College (1824, Gambier) is one of Ohio's oldest colleges — archives hold local records. Knox County Historical Society. Kokosing River valley. Johnny Appleseed territory — nursery and orchard land records. Strong Methodist and Episcopalian church records. Congress Lands survey system. Mount Vernon was home to Dan Emmett, credited with writing 'Dixie.'

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1808. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Painesville. Created 1840 from Geauga and Cuyahoga. Western Reserve county — check Connecticut records for early settlers. Lake Erie shoreline county. Finnish immigrant community in Fairport Harbor — Finnish church and fraternal records. Lake County Historical Society in Mentor. James A. Garfield National Historic Site. Morley Library (Painesville) local history collection. Check Geauga and Cuyahoga for pre-1840 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1840. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1840. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Connecticut Western Reserve Land Records — Lake County
source_profiles.repository: Western Reserve Historical Society, Cleveland / CT State Library
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Connecticut ceded its Western Reserve to the federal government in 1800, but settlers arrived from 1796. Connecticut Land Company records document original purchasers and settlers. Check Connecticut state records for settler origins.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at WRHS in Cleveland; Connecticut State Library also has copies

source_profiles.repository: Lake County Clerk of Courts, Painesville / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Court of Common Pleas. Post-1906 copies at NARA. Ohio had massive European immigration — German, Irish, Welsh, English, Italian, Hungarian, Slovak, Polish, Czech, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Ohio naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Ironton. Located on the Ohio River. Major iron furnace region (Hanging Rock Iron Region) — furnace records document workers from 1826. Lawrence County Historical Society. Underground Railroad routes along the river. Ironton-Russell Bridge area connecting to Kentucky. Check Gallia and Scioto for pre-1815 records. Charcoal iron industry attracted Welsh and English furnace workers. Ohio University Southern campus.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1815. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.source_name: Hanging Rock Iron Region Records — Lawrence County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / Local historical societies
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Charcoal iron furnace records including worker rolls, company store ledgers, and furnace operation logs. The Hanging Rock Iron Region in southern Ohio employed thousands of workers including Welsh, English, and African American laborers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered — some at Ohio History Connection, some at local museums (e.g., Buckeye Furnace State Memorial)

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Newark. Created 1808 from Fairfield. Newark Earthworks (Hopewell culture) — no genealogical records but historical context. Located in Congress Lands. Licking County Genealogical Society is very active. Denison University (1831, Granville) archives. Granville was settled by New Englanders from Granville, Massachusetts — check MA records. Heisey Glass factory records. Extensive early deed records from 1808.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1808. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Canal construction records, land purchases, worker rolls, and canal-related property records. The Ohio & Erie Canal (Cleveland to Portsmouth) and Miami & Erie Canal (Cincinnati to Toledo) transformed Ohio's economy and brought many immigrants.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Canal Commission records at Ohio History Connection

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio provided approximately 320,000 soldiers to the Union Army — third most of any state. Roster Commission records, enlistment papers, discharge records, and pension files. Ohio History Connection holds the Ohio Roster Commission records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Ohio Roster Commission published 'Official Roster of the Soldiers of the State of Ohio in the War of the Rebellion' (12 volumes)

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Bellefontaine. Located in Congress Lands. Campbell Hill is Ohio's highest point — early survey records. Bellefontaine had the first concrete street in America (1891). Logan County Historical Society. Zanesfield area is named for Isaac Zane (adopted Wyandot). Mac-A-Cheek and Mac-O-Chee castles area. Strong early Methodist circuit rider records. Check Champaign County for pre-1817 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1817. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1817. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.

source_profiles.source_name: Virginia Military District Land Warrants — Logan County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / Library of Virginia / BLM-GLO
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Virginia military bounty land warrants issued to Virginia Revolutionary War veterans. The Virginia Military District covered south-central Ohio between the Scioto and Little Miami Rivers. Warrants often bought and sold — chain of ownership can trace families. BLM-GLO has original federal patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records survive at multiple repositories — Ohio History Connection, Library of Virginia, and NARA

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio provided approximately 320,000 soldiers to the Union Army — third most of any state. Roster Commission records, enlistment papers, discharge records, and pension files. Ohio History Connection holds the Ohio Roster Commission records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Ohio Roster Commission published 'Official Roster of the Soldiers of the State of Ohio in the War of the Rebellion' (12 volumes)

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Elyria. Western Reserve county (partially). Oberlin College (1833) was the first coeducational and one of the first to admit African American students — extensive records in Oberlin College Archives. Lorain steel mill records document immigrant workers: Puerto Rican, Hungarian, Slovak, Polish, and Croatian communities. Lorain County Historical Society. Black River area. Elyria founded by Heman Ely of West Springfield, CT.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1822. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Toledo. Toledo is a major port city on Lake Erie and Maumee River. Toledo-Lucas County Public Library has excellent local history and genealogy collections. University of Toledo archives. Glass industry records (Libbey-Owens, Toledo Glass Co.) document worker families. Toledo-War (1835) with Michigan over the Toledo Strip — boundary dispute records. Strong Polish, Hungarian, and Lebanese immigrant communities. Lucas County Chapter OGS. Maumee Valley Historical Society.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in London. Located in the Virginia Military District — VA bounty land warrants. Created 1810 from Franklin County. Madison County Historical Society. London Correctional Institution records (state prison, 1924). Agricultural county — extensive early land records. Check Franklin County for pre-1810 records. Darby Creek area — significant prairie remnant. Camp Chase prisoner overflow area during Civil War.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Youngstown. Created 1846 from Columbiana and Trumbull. Major steel industry center — Youngstown Sheet & Tube, Republic Steel, and U.S. Steel employee records. Massive Italian, Slovak, Croatian, Welsh, and African American communities. Mahoning Valley Historical Society (Arms Museum). Youngstown State University archives. Western Reserve county. Steel industry decline records (1977 'Black Monday' onward). Check Columbiana and Trumbull for pre-1846 records.
